subject: Mathematics
solution:
$a, b, c \in N \quad a < b < c$
$\overline{\mathrm{x}}=$ mean $=\frac{9+25+\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}}{5}=18$
$a+b+c=56$
Mean deviation $=\frac{\Sigma\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\overline{\mathrm{x}}\right|}{\mathrm{n}}=4$
$\begin{aligned} & =9+7+|18-\mathrm{a}|+|18-\mathrm{b}|+|18-\mathrm{c}|=20 \\ & =|18-\mathrm{a}|+|18-\mathrm{b}|+|18-\mathrm{c}|=4\end{aligned}$
Variance $=\frac{\Sigma\left|\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}-\overline{\mathrm{x}}\right|^2}{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{136}{5}$
$\begin{aligned} & =81+49+|18-a|^2+|18-b|^2+|18-c|^2=136 \\ & =(18-a)^2+(18-b)^2+(18-c)^2=6\end{aligned}$
Possible values $(18-\mathrm{a})^2=1, \quad(18-\mathrm{b})^2=1 \quad(18-\mathrm{c})^2=4$ $\mathrm{a} < \mathrm{b} < \mathrm{c}$
so $\quad \begin{array}{ccc}18-a=1 & 18-b=-1 & 18-c=-2 \\ \mathrm{a}=17 & \mathrm{~b}=19 & \mathrm{c}=20\end{array}$
$a+b+c=56$
$2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{c} 34=19-20=33$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given 10 observations with mean = 9 and variance = 34.2.
Eight known values: 2, 3, 5, 10, 11, 13, 15, 21.
Sum of all 10: $\sum x_i = 90$.
Sum of 8 known: 80. So the two unknowns sum to 10.
From variance: $\sum x_i^2 = 10(34.2 + 81) = 1152$.
Sum of squares of 8 known: 1094. So unknown squares sum to 58.
With $x_9 + x_{10} = 10$ and $x_9^2 + x_{10}^2 = 58$: we get $x_9 x_{10} = 21$, giving roots $t^2 - 10t + 21 = 0$, so the unknowns are 3 and 7.
All 10 sorted: 2, 3, 3, 5, 7, 7, 10, 11, 13, 15, 21.
Median = $\frac{7+10}{2} = 8.5$.
Mean deviation = $\frac{1}{10}(6.5 + 5.5 + 5.5 + 3.5 + 1.5$ $+ 1.5 + 2.5 + 4.5 + 6.5 + 12.5)$ $= \frac{50}{10} = 5$

So, variance =40−36=4

∴ SD=2

solution:
$X=$ denotes number of defective
$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline \mathrm{x} & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x}) & \frac{7}{15} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{1}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & 1 & 4 & 9 \\
\hline \mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1{ }^2 & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{20}{12} & \frac{9}{12} \\
\hline \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}} & 0 & \frac{5}{12} & \frac{10}{12} & \frac{3}{12} \\
\hline
\end{array}$
$\mu=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{18}{12}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2=\frac{34}{12} \\
& \sigma^2=\Sigma \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{x}_1^2-(\mu)^2
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{34}{12}-\left(\frac{18}{12}\right)^2=\frac{17}{6}-\frac{9}{4} \\ & \frac{34-27}{12}=\frac{7}{12} \\ & 96 \sigma^2=96 \times \frac{7}{12}=56\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Class Frequency  Mid-point xi fixi
0-6 a 0+62=3 3a
6-12 b 6+122=9 9b
12-18 12 12+182=15 180
18-24 9 18+242=21 189
24-30 5 24+302=27 135
  N=26+a+b   504+3a+9b

We know that, mean=∑fixi∑fi

Given, mean=30922

⇒3a+9 b+180+189+135a+b+26=30922

⇒504+3a+9 ba+b+26=30922

⇒66a+198 b+11088=309a+309 b+8034

⇒243a+111 b=3054

⇒81a+37b=1018   ...1

Now, median=14, which lies in the interval 12-18, thus 12-18 is the median class and we have =l+N2-c×hf,

Where, l is the lower limit of the median class i.e. l=12, c is the cumulative frequency of the class above the median class i.e. c=a+b, f is the frequency of the median class i.e. f=12 and h is the length of the interval i.e. h=6.

Thus, we have 12+a+b+262-(a+b)12×6=14

⇒a+b+26-2a-2b22=14-12

⇒26-a-b4=2

⇒26-a-b=8

⇒a+b=18   ...2

On solving the equations 1 and 2, we get a=8, b=10.

∴ a-b2=8-102=4.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given for the first set: $n_1 = 4$, $\bar{x}_1 = 1$, $\sigma_1^2 = 13$.

$\sigma_1^2 = \dfrac{\sum x_i^2}{n_1} - (\bar{x}_1)^2$

$13 = \dfrac{\sum x_i^2}{4} - 1^2 \Rightarrow \sum x_i^2 = 56$

Also, $\sum x_i = n_1 \bar{x}_1 = 4 \times 1 = 4$

Given for the second set: $n_2 = 6$, $\bar{x}_2 = 2$, $\sigma_2^2 = 1$.

$\sigma_2^2 = \dfrac{\sum y_i^2}{n_2} - (\bar{x}_2)^2$

$1 = \dfrac{\sum y_i^2}{6} - 2^2 \Rightarrow \sum y_i^2 = 30$

Also, $\sum y_i = n_2 \bar{x}_2 = 6 \times 2 = 12$

For the combined set of $10$ observations:

Total sum $\sum z_i = \sum x_i + \sum y_i = 4 + 12 = 16$

Combined mean $\bar{z} = \dfrac{16}{10} = 1.6$

Total sum of squares $\sum z_i^2 = \sum x_i^2 + \sum y_i^2 = 56 + 30 = 86$

Combined variance $\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum z_i^2}{10} - (\bar{z})^2$

$\sigma^2 = \dfrac{86}{10} - (1.6)^2 = 8.6 - 2.56 = 6.04$

Answer: $6.04$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given $\sum_{i=1}^{20}(x_i + 5)^2 = 2500$ and $\sum_{i=1}^{20}(x_i - 5)^2 = 100$.

Expanding the first equation:
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} (x_i^2 + 10x_i + 25) = 2500$
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 + 10 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i + 500 = 2500$
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 + 10 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i = 2000$ --- (1)

Expanding the second equation:
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} (x_i^2 - 10x_i + 25) = 100$
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 - 10 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i + 500 = 100$
$\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 - 10 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i = -400$ --- (2)

Subtracting (2) from (1):
$20 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i = 2400 \Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i = 120$

Adding (1) and (2):
$2 \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 = 1600 \Rightarrow \sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2 = 800$

Mean ($\mu$) is given by:
$\mu = \dfrac{\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i}{20} = \dfrac{120}{20} = 6$

Variance ($\sigma^2$) is given by:
$\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum_{i=1}^{20} x_i^2}{20} - \mu^2 = \dfrac{800}{20} - 6^2 = 40 - 36 = 4$

Standard deviation ($\sigma$) is $\sqrt{4} = 2$.

The ratio of mean to standard deviation is:
$\dfrac{\mu}{\sigma} = \dfrac{6}{2} = \dfrac{3}{1}$

Answer: $3:1$

solution:
Variance of a set of n numbers x1, x2 ...xi, ...xn is given

σ2=1nΣi= 1nxi-x¯2, where x¯ is the average of the numbers.

We want the variance of 2, 4, 6, 8 ..... 48, 50, 52, ..... 98, 100

Here x¯=51.

xi-x¯ will be symmetric about 51,

∴σ2=25012+32+52+... 492

Now, 12 + 32 + 52 + ... + 492 = 12 + 22 + 32 + ... + 492 + 502 - 22 + 42 + 62 + ... + 482 + 502

=50511016-412+22+

=50511016-42526516

 = 42925 - 22100

 = 20825

∴σ2=250×20825=833

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Class midpoints: $6, 10, 14, 18$.
Frequencies: $3, \lambda, 4, 7$. $N = 14 + \lambda$.
Mean $\mu = \frac{200 + 10\lambda}{14 + \lambda}$.
$\sum f_i x_i^2 = 3(36) + 100\lambda + 4(196) + 7(324) = 3160 + 100\lambda$.
Variance: $19 = \frac{3160+100\lambda}{N} - \mu^2$.
Let $u = 14+\lambda$:
$19u^2 = u(1760+100u) - (60+10u)^2 \Rightarrow 19u^2 - 560u + 3600 = 0$.
$u = \frac{560 \pm 200}{38}$, so $u = 20 \Rightarrow \lambda = 6$.
$\mu = \frac{260}{20} = 13$. Hence $\lambda + \mu = 6 + 13 = 19$.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Mean = 8 gives $x + y = 14$.
Variance = 16 gives $(x-8)^2 + (y-8)^2 = 4$.
Solving: $x = 8$, $y = 6$ (since $x > y$).
Set becomes $\{1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 5\} = \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6\}$.
Total ways to choose 2 numbers = $\binom{6}{2} = 15$.
Favorable cases (smaller $< 4$): smaller = 1 gives 5 pairs, smaller = 2 gives 4 pairs, smaller = 3 gives 3 pairs.
Total favorable = 12.
Probability $= \frac{12}{15} = \frac{4}{5}$.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Clearly mean $\mathrm{A}=0$
Now, standard deviation $\sigma=\sqrt{\frac{\Sigma(x-\mathrm{A})^2}{2 n}}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
2=& \sqrt{\frac{(a-0)^2+(a-0)^2+\ldots \ldots+(0-a)^2+\ldots . .}{2 n}} \\
&=\sqrt{\frac{a^2 \cdot 2 n}{2 n}}=|a|
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, $|a|=2$
